---
title: "{{TITLE}}"
subtitle: "Scoping review protocol"
keywords:
  - scoping review
  - protocol
  - PRISMA-ScR
abstract: |
  **Introduction:** Background and rationale. State the problem the
  scoping review will map. Reference the most relevant prior work (one
  or two sentences).

  **Methods and analysis:** Brief overview of the PCC framework
  (Population, Concept, Context), search strategy, screening process,
  data charting plan, and synthesis approach.

  **Dissemination:** How the results will be reported (this preprint,
  the eventual peer-reviewed manuscript, conference presentations,
  partner organisations).

  **Conclusion:** One sentence summarising the contribution.

  **Keywords:** scoping review; protocol; PRISMA-ScR; (replace with
  topic-specific terms)

  **Ethics and Dissemination:** Scoping reviews of secondary literature
  do not require ethics approval. Findings will be disseminated via
  peer-reviewed publication and conference presentation.

version-history:
  - version: "0.1.0"
    date: "{{DATE}}"
    description: "Initial draft"
---

::: version-history
:::

::: author-plate
:::

::: toc
:::

# Article summary

## Strengths and limitations

- Strength: structured PRISMA-ScR-aligned protocol with a-priori PCC
  framework, eligibility criteria, and search strategy.
- Strength: methodological transparency — protocol pre-registered before
  screening begins.
- Limitation: scoping reviews provide breadth of coverage, not effect
  estimates — risk of bias is not formally assessed.
- Limitation: (add review-specific limitations)

# 1. Introduction

## 1.1 Rationale

Why this scoping review is needed. Cite key prior work: existing
reviews, gaps they did not cover, and how this review extends them.
Define the practical or policy-relevant question motivating the work.

## 1.2 Objective

State the overall objective in one sentence.

### 1.2.1 Research questions

1. (Primary research question, framed in PCC terms)
2. (Secondary question, if any)
3. (Methodological question, if any)

# 2. Methods

## 2.1 Approach

This protocol follows the PRISMA-ScR framework [@tricco2018] and the
JBI methodology for scoping reviews [@peters2020]. The review is
registered on the Open Science Framework at <OSF URL>.

## 2.2 Study scope

State the scope using the PCC framework:

- **Population:** (who or what)
- **Concept:** (the central concept being mapped)
- **Context:** (geographic, temporal, setting boundaries)

## 2.3 Eligibility criteria

### 2.3.1 Inclusion criteria

- Population: (specify)
- Concept: (specify)
- Context: (specify)
- Study designs: (specify or "any")
- Language: (specify, typically English)
- Publication date: (specify range)

### 2.3.2 Exclusion criteria

- Studies that do not meet the inclusion criteria
- Conference abstracts without full text available
- Editorials, commentaries, and opinion pieces (unless framed as
  methodological contributions)
- (Add review-specific exclusions)

## 2.4 Identifying relevant studies

### 2.4.1 Search strategy

The search strategy was developed with an information specialist using
controlled vocabulary (MeSH, Emtree) and free-text terms. The strategy
was peer-reviewed using the PRESS checklist [@mcgowan2016].

The full search strategy is available in `supplements/search-strategy.json`.

### 2.4.2 Database search

Databases to be searched:

- MEDLINE (Ovid)
- Embase (Ovid)
- Web of Science Core Collection
- (Add or remove databases as appropriate)

### 2.4.3 Grey literature

(Specify grey-literature sources, if any: institutional websites,
preprint servers, government reports, conference proceedings.)

### 2.4.4 Hand searching

(Specify journals to be hand-searched, if any.)

### 2.4.5 Reference checking

Reference lists of all included studies and relevant systematic
reviews will be screened for additional eligible studies.

## 2.5 Study selection

### 2.5.1 Title and abstract screening

Titles and abstracts will be screened independently by two reviewers
against the eligibility criteria. Disagreements will be resolved by
discussion or by a third reviewer. (Specify any AI-assisted screening
methodology and validation here, with detailed methods in an appendix.)

### 2.5.2 Full-text screening

Studies appearing potentially eligible will be retrieved in full and
screened independently by two reviewers. Reasons for exclusion at the
full-text stage will be recorded.

### 2.5.3 Reporting exclusions and PRISMA flow

A PRISMA-ScR flow diagram will report the number of records identified,
screened, assessed for eligibility, and included, with reasons for
exclusion at each stage.

## 2.6 Data charting

A data charting form will be developed iteratively. The charting form
template is in `supplements/data-charting-fields.csv`. Two reviewers
will independently chart data from a calibration sample (10 studies)
and agreement will be discussed before full charting begins.

Fields charted will include (at minimum):

- Bibliographic information (citation, year, country)
- Study design
- Population characteristics
- Concept-relevant data items
- Context-relevant data items
- (Add review-specific fields)

## 2.7 Synthesis of results

Results will be synthesised narratively, with tables summarising study
characteristics and findings against the research questions. Where
appropriate, frequency counts and proportions will quantify thematic
patterns. The synthesis will not pool effect estimates.

# 3. Dissemination

Findings will be disseminated through:

- This preprint (medRxiv, OSF Preprints)
- A peer-reviewed manuscript
- (Add: conference presentations, partner reports, lay summaries)

# 4. Ethics

This review uses only published secondary literature. Ethics committee
approval is not required.

# 5. Funding

(Funding statement. If none, state "This work received no specific
funding.")

# 6. Competing interests

(Competing interests statement.)

# 7. Author contributions

Author contributions follow the CRediT taxonomy (see `_quarto.yml`).

# 8. Data availability

The protocol, search strategy, screening prompts (if applicable), and
data charting form are deposited on OSF at <OSF URL>. The full review
data and code will be made available alongside the final manuscript.

# References

::: bibliography
:::
